1:38 PM

Claude responded: Looking at your Nursing TV dropdown, I can see the issue — it's quite narrow and cramped.

Looking at your Nursing TV dropdown, I can see the issue — it's quite narrow and cramped. Here are the main things to fix:

1. Make the dropdown wider The panel looks like it's around 500px. Bump it to min-w-[700px] or w-[750px] so the two channel cards have breathing room side by side.

2. Give the channel cards more padding and space The NCLEX-RN TV and NCLEX-PN TV cards look squeezed. Add more p-4 or p-5 padding inside each card, and increase the gap-4 between them to gap-6.

3. Expand the tab row The Licensure / Specialty / Entrance / Life Support tabs look tight. Add px-4 py-2 to each tab and more gap-2 or gap-4 between them.

4. Add more vertical padding to the whole dropdown The top (with "NURSING TV · 10 channels...") and bottom ("10 Channels · 140+ Lessons · Free Forever") rows need more py-4 or py-5 to stop looking squished.

To help you fix the exact code, can you share the file that contains your Nursing TV dropdown? It's likely in one of these:

  • components/Navbar.tsx
  • components/NursingTVDropdown.tsx
  • components/navbar/NursingTVMenu.tsx

Run this in PowerShell to find it:

powershell
Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter "*.tsx" | Select-String "Nursing TV" | Select-Object -First 10

Paste the result and I'll give you the exact updated code.

PS C:\Users\USER\Desktop\nclex-app> Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter "*.tsx" | Select-String "Nursing TV" | Select-Object -First 10 app\nursing-tv\page.tsx:170: <span style={{ fontSize: "10px", fontWeight: 800, color: "#ef4444", letterSpacing: ".22em" }}>NURSING TV</span> components\Navbar.tsx:335: Nursing TV components\Navbar.tsx:347: <span style={{ fontSize: "9px", fontWeight: 800, color: "#ef4444", letterSpacing: ".2em" }}>NURSING TV</span> components\Navbar.tsx:589: { href: "/nursing-tv", label: "Nursing TV", desc: "Video lessons by licensed RNs", icon: (<svg width="18" height="18" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M22 10v6M2 10l10-5 10 5-10 5z"/><path d="M6 12v5c3 3 9 3 12 0v-5"/></svg>), color: "#ef4444" }, components\Navbar.tsx:726: { href: "/nursing-tv", label: "Nursing TV" }, PS C:\Users\USER\Desktop\nclex-app>

The dropdown is in components/Navbar.tsx around line 335. Let me see that section:

1:39 PM

Claude responded: I can see the issue clearly.

I can see the issue clearly. The dropdown width is only 560px and the left offset is -220px, making it feel cramped. Here are the exact changes to make it wider and more spacious:

Edited a file, ran a command

The file isn't uploaded here — I'll give you the exact replacements to make in PowerShell directly. Make 3 small changes in components/Navbar.tsx:

Change 1 — Wider dropdown + better left offset:

Find this line (~line 347):

tsx
left: "-220px", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "560px"

Replace with:

tsx
left: "-300px", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "700px"

Change 2 — More padding on the header:

Find:

tsx
padding: "14px 20px", borderBottom:

Replace with:

tsx
padding: "16px 24px", borderBottom:

Change 3 — More padding and gap on the channel cards grid:

Find:

tsx
padding: "14px", background: "#ffffff", display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: "6px"

Replace with:

tsx
padding: "16px 20px", background: "#ffffff", display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: "10px"

Change 4 — More padding on each channel card:

Find:

tsx
padding: "11px 13px", borderRadius: "12px",

Replace with:

tsx
padding: "14px 16px", borderRadius: "12px",

After all four changes, run:

powershell
git add . && git commit -m "Widen Nursing TV dropdown" && git push

This will take the dropdown from 560px → 700px wide and give everything much more breathing room. The cards will feel balanced rather than squeezed.

1:42 PM

Claude responded: Perfect, I can see everything clearly.

Perfect, I can see everything clearly. I'll make 4 targeted fixes — wider dropdown, more padding on header, tabs, cards, and footer. Run each command one at a time:

Fix 1 — Wider dropdown + better position:

powershell
(Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw) -replace 'left: "-220px", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "560px"', 'left: "-300px", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "700px"' | Set-Content components\Navbar.tsx -NoNewline

Fix 2 — More padding on header:

powershell
(Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw) -replace 'padding: "14px 20px", borderBottom:', 'padding: "18px 24px", borderBottom:' | Set-Content components\Navbar.tsx -NoNewline

Fix 3 — More padding + gap on channels grid:

powershell
(Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw) -replace 'padding: "14px", background: "#ffffff", display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: "6px"', 'padding: "16px 20px", background: "#ffffff", display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: "12px"' | Set-Content components\Navbar.tsx -NoNewline

Fix 4 — More padding on each channel card:

powershell
(Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw) -replace 'padding: "11px 13px", borderRadius: "12px"', 'padding: "14px 16px", borderRadius: "12px"' | Set-Content components\Navbar.tsx -NoNewline

Run all four, then deploy:

powershell
git add . && git commit -m "Widen Nursing TV dropdown spacing" && git push

Let me know once done and share a screenshot — I'll check if it looks right!

Claude responded: The error is a UTF-8 encoding issue — the -NoNewline flag in PowerShell sometimes writes files with bad encoding.

The error is a UTF-8 encoding issue — the -NoNewline flag in PowerShell sometimes writes files with bad encoding. Let's fix it. Run this to check:

powershell
Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx | Select-Object -Index (338..342 | ForEach-Object { $_ })

Paste the output so I can see if the text looks garbled around the edited lines.

You said: PS C:\Users\USER\Desktop\nclex-app> Get-Content components\Navbar.

PS C:\Users\USER\Desktop\nclex-app> Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx | Select-Object -Index (338..342 | ForEach-Object { $_ }) <div className="nav-dropdown" style={{ position: "absolute", left: "-300px", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "700px" }}> <div style={{ background: "#ffffff", border: "1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.08)", borderRadius: "20px", boxShadow: "0 32px 80px rgba(0,0,0,.18)", overflow: "hidden" }}> {/* HEADER */} <div style={{ padding: "18px 24px", borderBottom: "1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.06)", background: "linear-gradient(135deg,rgba(239,68,68,.05),rgba(14,165,233,.04))", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "space-between" }}> PS C:\Users\USER\Desktop\nclex-app>

1:46 PM

Claude responded: The text looks fine — the issue is that Set-Content -NoNewline corrupted the file encoding.

The text looks fine — the issue is that Set-Content -NoNewline corrupted the file encoding. Fix it by re-saving with correct UTF-8 encoding:

powershell
$content = Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw -Encoding UTF8; [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))

Then deploy:

powershell
git add . ; git commit -m "Fix Navbar encoding" ; git push

Paste the output!

Claude responded: That ran fine — but notice it looks like you may have copied the command with the hyperlink formatting (System.

That ran fine — but notice it looks like you may have copied the command with the hyperlink formatting ([System.IO](http://System.IO)) from my message. That's a markdown rendering artifact.

The correct raw command to always use is:

powershell
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))

Make sure when you copy it, it reads System.IO.File not [System.IO](http://System.IO).File.

Now check your live site — has Vercel finished deploying? Share a screenshot of the Nursing TV dropdown!

Claude responded: You're right, sorry about that.

You're right, sorry about that. From now on, every edit will follow this single safe pattern — read, modify in memory, write back with correct UTF-8, then deploy. All in one block.

For any future edit, the template will always be:

powershell
$content = Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw -Encoding UTF8
$content = $content -replace 'OLD TEXT', 'NEW TEXT'
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
git add . ; git commit -m "message" ; git push

Multiple replacements in one shot, one deploy. No back and forth. Now — share a screenshot of the dropdown and I'll prep the next fix as one clean block.

Claude responded: The file is already corrupted from the earlier encoding issues.

The file is already corrupted from the earlier encoding issues. I can see two problems:

  1. ï¿½ garbage characters where · (middle dots) should be
  2. [groups.map](http://groups.map) — a hyperlink corrupted the code

The good news is the earlier fixes are already in (-300px, 700px, 18px 24px). But we need to fix the corruption first. Run this to restore clean characters:

powershell
$content = Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw -Encoding UTF8
$content = $content -replace 'ï¿½', '·'
$content = $content -replace '\[groups\.map\]\(http://groups\.map\)', 'groups.map'
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))

Claude responded: The replacements aren't matching because the corruption characters aren't being read correctly by PowerShell.

I can see two corrupted lines clearly. The issue is your terminal is rendering the bad characters. Let's fix both using Unicode escape codes:

$content = $content -replace '10 channels .{1,6} 140\+ lessons .{1,6} Free', '10 channels · 140+ lessons · Free'
$content = $content -replace 'groups\[activeGroup\]\.\[channels\.map\]\(http://channels\.map\)', 'groups[activeGroup].channels.map'
$content = $content -replace '\[groups\.map\]\(http://groups\.map\)', 'groups.map'
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))

1:56 PM

Claude responded: The problem is clear now — Claude's chat interface is corrupting the replacement strings too, converting groups.

The problem is clear now — Claude's chat interface is corrupting the replacement strings too, converting groups.map into [groups.map](http://groups.map) even in the commands I send you. So the replace source and target end up identical — nothing changes.

The only clean fix is to rewrite that section of the file directly in VS Code:

  1. Open components/Navbar.tsx in VS Code
  2. Press Ctrl+H (Find & Replace)
  3. Find: {[groups.map](http://groups.map)((g, i) => (
  4. Replace with: {groups.map((g, i) => (
  5. Then find: {groups[activeGroup].[channels.map](http://channels.map)(c => (
  6. Replace with: {groups[activeGroup].channels.map(c => (
  7. Save the file (Ctrl+S)

Then in the terminal:

powershell
git add . ; git commit -m "Fix corrupted map calls" ; git push

This bypasses the Claude chat rendering issue entirely. Let me know when done!

  3. In the Replace box paste: {groups.map((g, i) => (
  4. Click Replace All
  5. Then in Find paste: {groups[activeGroup].[channels.map](http://channels.map)(c => (
  6. In Replace paste: {groups[activeGroup].channels.map(c => (
  7. Click Replace All
  8. Press Ctrl+S to save

Claude responded: You're absolutely right.

You're absolutely right. Instead of guessing left offsets, the proper solution is to use right: "0" which anchors the dropdown to the right edge of the button — no guessing needed, it always stays within the navbar regardless of screen size.

powershell
$content = Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw -Encoding UTF8
$content = $content -replace 'left: "-160px", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "700px"', 'right: "0", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "680px"'
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
git add . ; git commit -m "Anchor Nursing TV dropdown to right edge" ; git push

This is the correct approach — right: 0 means the dropdown's right edge always aligns with the button's right edge, perfectly contained within the viewport every time.

2:06 PM

Claude responded: Looking at both dropdowns side by side, the Nursing TV dropdown needs these specific improvements to match the quality of the Features dropdown:

Looking at both dropdowns side by side, the Nursing TV dropdown needs these specific improvements to match the quality of the Features dropdown:

  1. Position — it's overflowing left off screen, needs to shift right
  2. Card height — the channel cards are too short/flat, need more vertical padding
  3. Overall height — the whole dropdown feels compressed vertically compared to Features

Here's the competent fix — using transform instead of left for precise centering, plus generous padding throughout:

powershell
$content = Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw -Encoding UTF8
$content = $content -replace 'right: "0", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "680px"', 'left: "50%", transform: "translateX(-50%)", top: "100%", zIndex: 1001, paddingTop: "10px", width: "720px"'
$content = $content -replace 'padding: "20px 24px", background: "#ffffff", display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: "14px"', 'padding: "24px 24px", background: "#ffffff", display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: "16px"'
$content = $content -replace 'padding: "18px 16px", borderRadius: "12px"', 'padding: "22px 18px", borderRadius: "12px"'
$content = $content -replace 'padding: "12px 16px", borderTop:', 'padding: "16px 24px", borderTop:'
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
git add . ; git commit -m "Redesign Nursing TV dropdown layout and spacing" ; git push

Paste the output!

Claude responded: Perfect, I have everything.

Perfect, I have everything. Here's a full premium redesign in one block — bigger icons, Cormorant Garamond headings, better typography, richer card design:

powershell
$content = Get-Content components\Navbar.tsx -Raw -Encoding UTF8
$content = $content -replace '(?s)\{/\* HEADER \*/\}.*?\{/\* GROUP TABS \*/\}', '{/* HEADER */}
            <div style={{ padding: "20px 28px", borderBottom: "1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.06)", background: "linear-gradient(135deg,rgba(239,68,68,.06),rgba(14,165,233,.05))", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "space-between" }}>
              <div style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "14px" }}>
                <div style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "7px", background: "rgba(239,68,68,0.08)", border: "1px solid rgba(239,68,68,0.2)", borderRadius: "100px", padding: "5px 14px" }}>
                  <span style={{ width: "6px", height: "6px", borderRadius: "50%", background: "#ef4444", display: "inline-block", boxShadow: "0 0 6px #ef4444" }} />
                  <span style={{ fontSize: "10px", fontWeight: 900, color: "#ef4444", letterSpacing: ".22em", fontFamily: "inherit" }}>NURSING TV</span>
                </div>
                <span style={{ fontSize: "12px", color: "#94a3b8", fontWeight: 500, letterSpacing: ".01em" }}>10 channels · 140+ lessons · Free</span>
              </div>
              <Link href="/nursing-tv" style={{ fontSize: "12px", fontWeight: 700, color: "#0ea5e9", textDecoration: "none", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "5px", letterSpacing: ".01em" }}>
                Browse all
                <svg width="12" height="12" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2.5" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M5 12h14M12 5l7 7-7 7"/></svg>
              </Link>
            </div>

            {/* GROUP TABS */}'
$content = $content -replace '(?s)\{/\* GROUP TABS \*/\}.*?\{/\* CHANNELS \*/\}', '{/* GROUP TABS */}
            <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: "2px", padding: "12px 20px 0", background: "#f8f9fb", borderBottom: "1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.06)" }}>
              {groups.map((g, i) => (
                <button key={g.label} onMouseEnter={() => setActiveGroup(i)}
                  style={{ padding: "9px 20px", borderRadius: "10px 10px 0 0", background: activeGroup === i ? "#ffffff" : "transparent", border: activeGroup === i ? "1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.08)" : "1px solid transparent", borderBottom: activeGroup === i ? "1px solid #fff" : "1px solid transparent", cursor: "pointer", fontFamily: "inherit", fontSize: "13px", fontWeight: activeGroup === i ? 700 : 500, color: activeGroup === i ? g.color : "#94a3b8", transition: "all .15s", marginBottom: "-1px", letterSpacing: ".01em" }}>
                  {g.label}
                </button>
              ))}
            </div>

            {/* CHANNELS */}'
$content = $content -replace '(?s)\{/\* CHANNELS \*/\}.*?\{/\* FOOTER \*/\}', '{/* CHANNELS */}
            <div style={{ padding: "24px", background: "#ffffff", display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: "14px" }}>
              {groups[activeGroup].channels.map(c => (
                <Link key={c.title} href="/nursing-tv"
                  style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "14px", padding: "18px 20px", borderRadius: "14px", textDecoration: "none", background: "#f8f9fb", border: "1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.06)", transition: "all .2s", position: "relative", overflow: "hidden" }}
                  onMouseEnter={e => { e.currentTarget.style.background = `${c.color}08`; e.currentTarget.style.borderColor = `${c.color}30`; e.currentTarget.style.transform = "translateY(-2px)"; e.currentTarget.style.boxShadow = `0 8px 24px ${c.color}15`; }}
                  onMouseLeave={e => { e.currentTarget.style.background = "#f8f9fb"; e.currentTarget.style.borderColor = "rgba(0,0,0,.06)"; e.currentTarget.style.transform = "translateY(0)"; e.currentTarget.style.boxShadow = "none"; }}>
                  <div style={{ position: "absolute", top: 0, left: 0, bottom: 0, width: "3px", background: `linear-gradient(180deg,${c.color},${c.color}88)`, borderRadius: "3px 0 0 3px" }} />
                  <div style={{ width: "42px", height: "42px", borderRadius: "12px", background: `${c.color}12`, border: `1px solid ${c.color}25`, display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", flexShrink: 0 }}>
                    <svg width="18" height="18" fill="none" stroke={c.color} strokeWidth="1.8" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M21.543 6.498C22 8.28 22 12 22 12s0 3.72-.457 5.502c-.254.985-.997 1.76-1.938 2.022C17.896 20 12 20 12 20s-5.893 0-7.605-.476c-.945-.266-1.687-1.04-1.938-2.022C2 15.72 2 12 2 12s0-3.72.457-5.502c.254-.985.997-1.76 1.938-2.022C6.107 4 12 4 12 4s5.896 0 7.605.476c.945.266 1.687 1.04 1.938 2.022zM10 15.5l6-3.5-6-3.5v7z"/></svg>
                  </div>
                  <div style={{ flex: 1, minWidth: 0 }}>
                    <div style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "7px", marginBottom: "4px" }}>
                      <p style={{ fontSize: "14px", fontWeight: 700, color: "#0f172a", margin: 0, fontFamily: "'Cormorant Garamond',serif", letterSpacing: ".01em" }}>{c.title}</p>
                      {c.live && <span style={{ fontSize: "8px", fontWeight: 800, background: "rgba(239,68,68,.1)", color: "#ef4444", border: "1px solid rgba(239,68,68,.2)", padding: "2px 7px", borderRadius: "100px", letterSpacing: ".12em" }}>LIVE</span>}
                    </div>
                    <p style={{ fontSize: "11px", color: "#64748b", margin: 0, letterSpacing: ".01em" }}>{c.tag} · {c.lessons} lessons</p>
                  </div>
                </Link>
              ))}
            </div>

            {/* FOOTER */}'
$content = $content -replace '(?s)\{/\* FOOTER \*/\}.*?</div>\s*</div>\s*</div>\s*\}\)', '{/* FOOTER */}
            <div style={{ padding: "16px 28px", borderTop: "1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.06)", background: "linear-gradient(135deg,rgba(239,68,68,.03),rgba(14,165,233,.03))", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "space-between" }}>
              <div style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "24px" }}>
                {[{ val: "10", label: "Channels" }, { val: "140+", label: "Lessons" }, { val: "Free", label: "Forever" }].map(s => (
                  <div key={s.label} style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "baseline", gap: "4px" }}>
                    <span style={{ fontSize: "18px", fontWeight: 800, color: "#0f172a", fontFamily: "'Cormorant Garamond',serif" }}>{s.val}</span>
                    <span style={{ fontSize: "11px", color: "#94a3b8", fontWeight: 500 }}>{s.label}</span>
                  </div>
                ))}
              </div>
              <Link href="/nursing-tv" style={{ display: "inline-flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "7px", background: "linear-gradient(135deg,#0ea5e9,#38bdf8)", color: "#fff", padding: "10px 22px", borderRadius: "10px", fontSize: "12px", fontWeight: 700, textDecoration: "none", boxShadow: "0 4px 16px rgba(14,165,233,.3)", letterSpacing: ".02em" }}>
                <svg width="11" height="11" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M8 5v14l11-7z"/></svg>
                Watch free
              </Link>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
})'
[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$PWD\components\Navbar.tsx", $content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))
npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | Select-Object -First 20

} function NursingTVDropdown({ pathname }: { pathname: string }) { const [open, setOpen] = useState(false); const [activeGroup, setActiveGroup] = useState(0); const timeout = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null); function handleEnter() { if (timeout.current) clearTimeout(timeout.current); setOpen(true); } function handleLeave() { timeout.current = setTimeout(() => setOpen(false), 200); } const groups = [ { label: "Licensure", color: "#0ea5e9", channels: [ { title: "NCLEX-RN TV", tag: "Registered Nurse", color: "#0ea5e9", lessons: 24, live: true }, { title: "NCLEX-PN TV", tag: "Practical Nurse", color: "#6366f1", lessons: 18, live: true }, ]}, { label: "Specialty", color: "#ef4444", channels: [ { title: "CCRN TV", tag: "Critical Care RN", color: "#ef4444", lessons: 20, live: true }, { title: "CEN TV", tag: "Emergency Nurse", color: "#f97316", lessons: 14, live: false }, { title: "PCCN TV", tag: "Progressive Care", color: "#ec4899", lessons: 12, live: false }, { title: "FNP TV", tag: "Nurse Practitioner", color: "#8b5cf6", lessons: 16, live: false }, ]}, { label: "Entrance", color: "#f59e0b", channels: [ { title: "TEAS 7 TV", tag: "Pre-Nursing Exam", color: "#f59e0b", lessons: 12, live: false }, { title: "HESI A2 TV", tag: "Admission Exam", color: "#10b981", lessons: 10, live: false }, ]}, { label: "Life Support", color: "#06b6d4", channels: [ { title: "ACLS TV", tag: "Advanced Cardiac", color: "#06b6d4", lessons: 8, live: false }, { title: "BLS TV", tag: "Basic Life Support", color: "#84cc16", lessons: 6, live: false }, ]}, ]; return ( <div style={{ position: "relative" }} onMouseEnter={handleEnter} onMouseLeave={handleLeave}> <button onClick={() => window.location.href="/nursing-tv"} className={nav-btn${isActive(pathname, "/nursing-tv") ? " active" : ""}} style={{ background: "none", border: "none", cursor: "pointer", fontFamily: "inherit" }}> <svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="#ef4444" style={{ flexShrink: 0 }}><path d="M21.543 6.498C22 8.28 22 12 22 12s0 3.72-.457 5.502c-.254.985-.997 1.76-1.938 2.022C17.896 20 12 20 12 20s-5.893 0-7.605-.476c-.945-.266-1.687-1.04-1.938-2.022C2 15.72 2 12 2 12s0-3.72.457-5.502c.254-.985.997-1.76 1.938-2.022C6.107 4 12 4 12 4s5.896 0 7.605.476c.945.266 1.687 1.04 1.938 2.022zM10 15.5l6-3.5-6-3.5v7z"/></svg> Nursing TV <ChevronDown style={{ width: 12, height: 12, transition: "transform .2s", transform: open ?
